2603.08359.title: Computational modeling of early language learning from acoustic speech and audiovisual input without linguistic priors
2603.08359.paper_summary: Learning to understand speech appears almost effortless for typically developing infants, yet from an information-processing perspective, acquiring a language from acoustic speech is an enormous challenge. This chapter reviews recent developments in using computational models to understand early language acquisition from speech and audiovisual input. The focus is on self-supervised and visually grounded models of perceptual learning. We show how these models are becoming increasingly powerful in learning various aspects of speech without strong linguistic priors, and how many features of early language development can be explained through a shared set of learning principles-principles broadly compatible with multiple theories of language acquisition and human cognition. We also discuss how modern learning simulations are gradually becoming more realistic, both in terms of input data and in linking model behavior to empirical findings on infant language development.
2603.08359.paper_summary_zh: 學習理解語音對於典型發展的嬰兒來說似乎幾乎是輕而易舉的，然而從信息處理的角度來看，從聲音語言中獲得語言是一個巨大的挑戰。這一章回顧了使用計算模型來理解語音和視聽輸入中早期語言習得的最新發展。重點在於自我監督和視覺基礎的感知學習模型。我們展示了這些模型在學習語音的各個方面變得越來越強大，而無需強烈的語言先驗知識，以及許多早期語言發展的特徵如何通過一組共享的學習原則來解釋——這些原則與多種語言習得和人類認知理論廣泛兼容。我們還討論了現代學習模擬如何在輸入數據和將模型行為與嬰兒語言發展的實證發現聯繫起來方面逐漸變得更加現實。
2603.08359.author: Okko Räsänen et.al.
